ddls:
CREATE TABLE "crime_incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date the incident was reported */,
  "incident_time" TEXT /* Time the incident occurred */,
  "suburb_id" INTEGER /* ID of the suburb where the incident occurred */,
  "postcode" INTEGER /* Postcode where the incident occurred */,
  "offence_level_1" TEXT /* Level 1 description of the offence (e.g., OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY) */,
  "offence_level_2" TEXT /* Level 2 description of the offence (e.g., PROPERTY DAMAGE AND ENVIRONMENTAL) */,
  "offence_level_3" TEXT /* Level 3 description of the offence (e.g., Property damage by fire or explosion) */,
  "offence_count" INTEGER /* Number of offences committed within the incident */,
  "incident_location" TEXT /* Specific location within the suburb */,
  "incident_status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident (e.g., Open, Closed, Under Investigation) */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* ID of the person who reported the incident */,
  "assigned_officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer assigned to the incident */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Details of how the incident was resolved */,
  "incident_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the officer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the officer */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer */,
  "badge_number" TEXT /* Badge number of the officer */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the officer */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the officer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporters" (
  "reporter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reporter */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the reporter */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the reporter */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the reporter */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the reporter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reporter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suburbs" (
  "suburb_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suburb */,
  "suburb_name" TEXT /* Name of the suburb */,
  "postcode" INTEGER /* Postcode of the suburb */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the suburb */,
  "area_sq_km" TEXT /* Area of the suburb in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suburb_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_types" (
  "crime_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime type */,
  "crime_type" TEXT /* Type of crime */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the crime type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "offence_levels" (
  "offence_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each offence level */,
  "level_1" TEXT /* Level 1 description of the offence */,
  "level_2" TEXT /* Level 2 description of the offence */,
  "level_3" TEXT /* Level 3 description of the offence */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the offence level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offence_level_id")
)
column_names: ID of the suburb where the incident occurred
Level 1 description of the offence (e.g., OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY)
Level 2 description of the offence (e.g., PROPERTY DAMAGE AND ENVIRONMENTAL)
Level 3 description of the offence (e.g., Property damage by fire or explosion)
Number of offences committed within the incident
Current status of the incident (e.g., Open, Closed, Under Investigation)
ID of the person who reported the incident
ID of the officer assigned to the incident
Date when the incident was resolved
Details of how the incident was resolved
Additional notes or comments about the incident

Unique identifier for each offence level
